steps.title: Set w(r) = 0 and isolate fractions
steps.content:
Starting with $w(r) = \frac{1}{r-6} - \frac{r-5}{-r+5.25} = 0$, we rearrange to isolate the fractions on opposite sides:
$$\frac{1}{r-6} = \frac{r-5}{-r+5.25}$$

steps.title: Simplify the denominator and cross-multiply
steps.content:
Rewrite the right denominator: $-r + 5.25 = -(r - 5.25)$, so:
$$\frac{1}{r-6} = -\frac{r-5}{r-5.25}$$
Cross-multiplying gives:
$$r - 5.25 = -(r-6)(r-5)$$

steps.title: Expand and form a quadratic equation
steps.content:
Expand the right side:
$$r - 5.25 = -(r^2 - 11r + 30)$$
$$r - 5.25 = -r^2 + 11r - 30$$
Rearranging to standard form:
$$r^2 - 10r + 24.75 = 0$$
Multiply by 4 to clear decimals: $4r^2 - 40r + 99 = 0$

steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula and find the greater root
steps.content:
Using the quadratic formula with $a = 4$, $b = -40$, $c = 99$:
$$r = \frac{40 \pm \sqrt{1600 - 1584}}{8} = \frac{40 \pm \sqrt{16}}{8} = \frac{40 \pm 4}{8}$$
This yields $r = \frac{44}{8} = \frac{11}{2}$ or $r = \frac{36}{8} = \frac{9}{2}$.
The greatest value is $\frac{11}{2}$ or $5.5$.

Equivalent forms accepted: 11/2, 5.5.
